message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in current code (6 passes hardened). confirmed=true only if real, reproducible, worth fixing (not speculative/by-design).
J-005: T3 transient error-classification tests are non-discriminating: assert base ReasoningError, so a transient->permanent/rate-limit misclassification still passes
File: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py:143-146, 168-171, 202-206
VERIFIED. The T3 drain classifier's purpose is to map an SDK error to the CORRECT subtype because the retry executor branches on exact subtype (PermanentReasoningError => no retry; RateLimitError => long backpressure; base ReasoningError => fast backoff). Both subtypes subclass ReasoningError. Three drain tests assert classification only via pytest.raises(ReasoningError) (the base class): test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient (145), test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient (a 500, 170), test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient (205). Since pytest.raises catches subclasses, these pass even if the code misclassifies — e.g. mapping a generic 500 to PermanentReasoningError (don't retry) or RateLimitError still satisfies the assertion. The transient-vs-permanent distinction these tests claim to guard is exactly what is NOT asserted. By contrast, the same file's specific tests correctly pin subtypes (test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent:164 -> PermanentReasoningError; test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit:182 -> RateLimitError), confirming an isolated weakness, not a convention.
PoC: In seam.py replace the generic fall-through `raise ReasoningError(...)` with `raise PermanentReasoningError(...)` (a real misclassification: a transient 500 becomes non-retryable). Re-run test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient — it still PASSES because PermanentReasoningError subclasses ReasoningError; the test fails to detect the 500 is no longer retried.
Fix: Make the transient assertions discriminating: capture the exception and assert it is exactly the base/transient type and not the specific subtypes, e.g. `with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei: _run(); assert type(ei.value) is ReasoningError; assert not isinstance(ei.value, (PermanentReasoningError, RateLimitError))` (or introduce a dedicated TransientReasoningError and assert it). Apply to all three tests so a transient->permanent/rate-limit regression fails every run.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	_HANG = object()  # sentinel: query awaits "forever" to simulate a hung subprocess (#127)
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
63	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream.
64	
65	    Exposes the captured ClaudeAgentOptions via ``scripted.captured_options`` so tests can assert
66	    the security wiring (env=scrubbed_env, sandbox flags) actually reaches the subprocess.
67	    """
68	    import anyio
69	
70	    class _Scripted(list):
71	        captured_options = None  # query records the ClaudeAgentOptions it received here
72	
73	    scripted = _Scripted()
74	
75	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
76	        scripted.captured_options = options
77	        for msg in scripted:
78	            if msg is _HANG:  # simulate a hung subprocess; inner fail_after must cancel us
79	                await anyio.sleep(3600)
80	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
81	                raise msg
82	            yield msg
83	
84	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
85	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
86	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
87	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
88	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
89	    mod.query = query
90	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
91	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
92	    return scripted
93	
94	
95	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
96	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
97	
98	
99	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
100	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
101	    assert _run() == "pong"
102	
103	
104	def test_t3_wires_scrubbed_env_into_subprocess_options(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
105	    # #124: the no-metered-API firewall must be wired end-to-end — env=scrubbed_env() must reach
106	    # the ClaudeAgentOptions handed to the subprocess (not just be unit-tested in isolation).
107	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "[REDACTED]")
108	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://attacker.example")
109	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
110	    _run()
111	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
112	    assert hasattr(opts, "env"), "env not wired into ClaudeAgentOptions (#124 firewall unguarded)"
113	    # the merged child env must neutralize the metered key + off-box redirect, keep OAuth
114	    child = {**__import__("os").environ, **opts.env}
115	    assert child["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] == "" and child["ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL"] == ""
116	    assert child["CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"] == "test-subscription-token"
117	
118	
119	def test_t3_wires_subprocess_sandbox_settings(fake_sdk):
120	    # #125: the subprocess sandbox flags must actually reach the options (no tools, no settings
121	    # sources, non-interactive permission mode).
122	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
123	    _run()
124	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
125	    assert opts.allowed_tools == []  # no tools may run in a reasoning call
126	    assert opts.setting_sources == []  # do not load ambient project/user settings
127	    assert opts.permission_mode == "dontAsk"  # non-interactive, deny-by-default
128	
129	
130	def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
131	    # #127: a hung stream must be cancelled by the inner timeout (frees the pool slot) and
132	    # surface a typed ReasoningError — not block forever.
133	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", 0.3)
134	    fake_sdk.append(_HANG)
135	    import time as _t
136	
137	    t0 = _t.monotonic()
138	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
139	        _run()
140	    assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
141	
142	
143	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
144	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
145	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):

147	
148

169	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
170	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
171	        _run()
172	
173	
174	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
175	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
176	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
177	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
178	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
179	        _run()
180	
181

183	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
184	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
185	        _run()
186	
187

189	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
190	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
191	        _run()

193	
194	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
195	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
196	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
197	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
198	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):

201	
202	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
203	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
204	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
205	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):

211	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)

content.text: Confirmed: both `PermanentReasoningError` and `RateLimitError` subclass `ReasoningError`. Now let me confirm the executor branches on exact subtype, so misclassification has real consequences.

585	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
586	    """
587	    transient = 0
588	    rate = 0
589	    attempted = False
590	    while True:
591	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
592	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
593	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
594	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
595	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
596	        attempted = True
597	        try:
598	            return _attempt(request)
599	        except PermanentReasoningError:
600	            raise
601	        except RateLimitError:
602	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
603	                raise
604	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
605	            rate += 1
606	        except ReasoningError:
607	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
608	                raise
609	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
610	            transient += 1

content.text:
Confirmed. The executor at lines 599-606 branches on exact subtype order: `PermanentReasoningError` re-raises (no retry), `RateLimitError` gets long backpressure backoff, base `ReasoningError` gets fast exponential backoff. The classification distinction is real and consequential.

The three tests at 143-146, 168-171, 202-206 assert only `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)`, which catches all subclasses. A regression mapping a transient 500 to `PermanentReasoningError` (no retry) or `RateLimitError` (wrong backoff) would still pass these tests. The PoC is valid. The contrast with the specific tests (auth->Permanent at 164, 529->RateLimit at 184) confirms this is an isolated weakness, not a deliberate convention.

This is a genuine test-quality gap that allows a real retry-behavior regression to go undetected. Severity is medium: it's a test weakness (latent risk) rather than an active production defect; the production code is currently correct.

input.reasoning:
Verified against actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

Class hierarchy (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:66-75): both PermanentReasoningError (line 70) and RateLimitError (line 74) subclass ReasoningError (line 66). So pytest.raises(ReasoningError) catches all three types.

Retry executor branches on EXACT subtype (seam.py:597-610): `except PermanentReasoningError: raise` (no retry), `except RateLimitError:` (long RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF backpressure, capped at RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES), `except ReasoningError:` (fast BACKOFF_BASE**transient exponential, capped at MAX_RETRIES). The transient-vs-permanent-vs-ratelimit distinction therefore has real, divergent runtime consequences (retry/no-retry and backoff strategy).

The three flagged tests assert only the base class:
- test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient (line 145): pytest.raises(ReasoningError)
- test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient (a 500, line 170): pytest.raises(ReasoningError)
- test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient (line 205): pytest.raises(ReasoningError)

Because pytest.raises catches subclasses, a misclassification (e.g. mapping a transient 500 to PermanentReasoningError -> not retried, or to RateLimitError -> wrong backoff) still passes. The PoC is sound: replacing the generic fall-through with PermanentReasoningError would keep these tests green while silently making a retryable 500 non-retryable. The same file's sibling tests correctly pin exact subtypes (auth 401/403 -> PermanentReasoningError lines 164/190; 529 overloaded -> RateLimitError line 184; rejected -> RateLimitError lines 152/178/198), confirming this is an isolated non-discriminating gap rather than an intentional convention.

This is a real, reproducible test-quality defect worth fixing: the tests claim to guard the transient classification but do not, so a transient->permanent/rate-limit regression would ship undetected. It is not speculative or by-design. Severity medium rather than high because current production classification code is correct; this is latent regression risk in the test suite, not an active runtime fault. Recommended fix as described: capture the exception and assert `type(ei.value) is ReasoningError` and `not isinstance(ei.value, (PermanentReasoningError, RateLimitError))` for all three tests (or introduce a dedicated TransientReasoningError type).

content.text: Verification complete. J-005 is confirmed as a true production-readiness problem (test-quality defect), severity medium. Findings returned via StructuredOutput.
